growth_records.medium: GG medium (Schatz medium; glutamate + gluconate as sole carbon sources)

up.method: 1H-NMR extracellular metabolomics; qS = mu/Yxs between T1(141h) and T3
1H-NMR extracellular metabolomics; qS = mu/Yxs between T1(141h) and T3

yields.note: Biomass obtained from OD600 using scaling factor 0.66 g/L per OD at 0°C; Yxs (biomass yield g/mol) used to derive qS but numeric value not tabulated in text

growth_records.cite: Riccardi, Christopher et al. Metabolic Robustness to Growth Temperature of a Cold-Adapted Marine Bacterium. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01124-22. [PMC10134870]
growth_records.method: reported (measured growth rate between T1 and T3 used to constrain/validate FBA; whole-period average mu was 0.016 h^-1)
growth_records.snippet: Average uptake rates of glutamate and gluconate ... resulted in 0.10 and 0.08 mmol/gCDW*h at 0°C and 0.47 and 0.62 mmol/gCDW*h at 15°C. ... at 0°C the results of this simulation (0.021h) were in line with the measured growth rates (0.027h).
growth_records.notes: Cold-adapted marine bacterium, GEM (constraint/validation) paper. Two carbon sources co-consumed (glutamate + gluconate), both experimental via 1H-NMR of spent medium. Uptake rates computed by authors as qS = mu/Yxs between T1(141 h) and T3; biomass from OD600 with scaling factor 0.66 g/L/OD at 0°C. Reported measured mu at 0°C = 0.027 h^-1 (T1-T3, used for FBA comparison; FBA predicted 0.021 h^-1). Whole-growth-period average mu = 0.016 h^-1 (s.d. 6.4e-05). Doubling time computed td = ln2/0.027 = 25.7 h. Medium (GG/Schatz) composition cited from ref 47, not restated -> composition empty. No excreted metabolic products reported (substrates depleted, no overflow secretion measured).

growth_records.medium: GG medium (Schatz medium; glutamate + gluconate as sole carbon sources)

up.method: 1H-NMR extracellular metabolomics; qS = mu/Yxs between T1(14h) and T3
1H-NMR extracellular metabolomics; qS = mu/Yxs between T1(14h) and T3

yields.note: Biomass obtained from OD600 using scaling factor 0.74 g/L per OD at 15°C; Yxs used to derive qS but numeric value not tabulated in text

growth_records.cite: Riccardi, Christopher et al. Metabolic Robustness to Growth Temperature of a Cold-Adapted Marine Bacterium. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01124-22. [PMC10134870]
growth_records.method: reported (measured growth rate between T1 and T3 used for FBA comparison; whole-period average mu was 0.11 h^-1)
growth_records.snippet: Average uptake rates of glutamate and gluconate ... 0.47 and 0.62 mmol/gCDW*h at 15°C. ... at 15°C a discrepancy was observed between the experimental growth rate and the one predicted in silico, 0.27 versus 0.13 h.
growth_records.notes: Same organism/strain, 15°C condition. Both carbon sources experimental via 1H-NMR of spent medium; qS = mu/Yxs between T1(14 h) and T3; biomass from OD600 with scaling factor 0.74 g/L/OD at 15°C. Reported measured mu at 15°C = 0.27 h^-1 (T1-T3; FBA predicted 0.13 h^-1, a discrepancy the authors flag). Whole-growth-period average mu = 0.11 h^-1 (s.d. 0.011). Doubling time td = ln2/0.27 = 2.57 h. Medium composition cited from ref 47, not restated. No overflow secretion products measured.

growth_records.medium: Schatz salts + 5 g/l Peptone N-Z-Soy BL 7 (complex medium)
growth_records.cite: Perrin E et al. Diauxie and co-utilization of carbon sources can coexist during bacterial growth in nutritionally complex environments. Nature Communications (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41467-020-16872-8. [PMC7305145]
growth_records.snippet: An initial growth phase (growth rate of 0.023 h-1, 0.021 considering interpolated data) is interrupted by a lag phase ... Cells then started growing again, until the end of the experiment (growth rate of 0.004 h-1, 0.006 h-1 ...). The avera
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (marine bacterium P. haloplanktis TAC125) grown as batch shaken-flask culture in Schatz salts + 5 g/l peptone at 21 C, aerobic. Growth is biphasic (diauxie interrupted by two lag phases). REPORTED growth rates: initial exponential phase mu=0.023 h-1 (0.021 with interpolated data); late phase mu=0.004 h-1 (0.006 interpolated); average across all time points mu=0.01 h-1. growth_rate_per_h field holds the initial exponential mu=0.023 (primary measured value). Doubling times (calculated td=ln2/mu): td(0.023)=0.693/0.023=30.1 h; td(0.004)=0.693/0.004=173.3 h; td(0.01 avg)=0.693/0.01=69.3 h. pH of the Schatz+peptone medium not stated in text (the separate 19-AA M9 experiments were pH 7.0, different medium). No quantitative uptake or secretion rates (mmol/gDW/h) are given in the main text: substrate consumption (amino acids) was tracked qualitatively by 1H NMR of spent medium (order of AA depletion) and product/biomass yields per single amino acid are reported only in Supplementary Figures (not in this XML), so uptake_rates/secretion_rates/yields left empty rather than fabricated. Qualitative note from text: growth rate on Leu is ~2-fold lower than on Ala; amino acids giving highest single-substrate growth rate/biomass yield are consumed first in the 19-AA experiment. Additional 19-AA-medium batch experiments (Schatz salts + 19 AA at 1 mM or 2 mM each, or 16 AA at 0.x mM; 21 C, aerobic) were run but lack in-text quantitative growth rates, so no separate records emitted.
